tags.qa_notes: Pinnacle confirmed by multiple sources. Bull shark claim removed (unconfirmed). Whale shark claim removed (unconfirmed — one source says manta rays). Hammerhead claim removed (single source only). Black-tip reef sharks and nurse sharks confirmed. Seasonal access confirmed (calm seas required, Aug-Nov best; also Sep-Oct per ScubaBoard). Depth 35-42m confirmed.

CORRECTED: site_type reef -> wall (multiple sources confirm wall dropping 12-16m). CORRECTED: depth 7m -> 16m. CORRECTED: location is northwest tip of Isla Solarte (not northern tip of Isla Colon). Coordinates 9.3348/-82.2198 correspond to NW Isla Solarte which is correct. Historical note: was United Fruit Company hospital complex (1901). Caverns/cracks in wall confirmed.

CORRECTED: depth 18m -> 22m (therealdealtours lists avg 22-45ft, max 72ft/22m). Bocas Dive Center notes it has swim-throughs and small caverns — not a plain reef. Site type kept as reef (primary character) but description updated. Hawksbill turtles confirmed Apr-Jun per therealdealtours.
Location on SE side of Isla Bastimentos confirmed. Max depth 60ft/18m confirmed. Coral garden with sponges confirmed. Barracuda mentioned by therealdealtours. Reef type confirmed.

CORRECTED: site_type cavern -> cave (multiple sources confirm extensive network of caverns, caves, and underwater tunnels — qualifies as cave). CORRECTED: depth 30m -> 12m (tribloo.com and therealdealtours.com both state max 12m/40ft, NOT 30m). CORRECTED: difficulty Advanced -> Intermediate (12m max, direct surface access throughout 90% of dive). CORRECTED: location on Isla Bastimentos (not 'northwestern tip of archipelago'). Seasonal: accessible July-November when water clarity improves. Name in data as 'Polo Beach Wall' but locally known as 'Polo Channel' or 'Polo Channels'.

No site-specific sources found for 'Dark Wood Reef' by this name across multiple searches. Site name does not appear in any dive operator listing, ScubaBoard thread, or dive database consulted. Cannot validate type, depth, or marine life. Retaining as unvalidated.

Confirmed shallow reef (max 18-20ft per therealdealtours, some sources say 60ft but that is for Crawl Cay). Note: therealdealtours lists 'Coral Cay' as max 60ft/18m but that entry references Crawl Cay / Coral Cay restaurant — there is some name confusion locally. Retaining 8m as the shallow snorkel/beginner depth for the cay-surrounding reef.

No independent source found for a dive site named 'Wreck Rock' in Bocas del Toro. The name does not appear in any dive operator listing, ScubaBoard thread, or dive database. The location (SW Isla Colon) is consistent with the Barco Hundido ferryboat wreck location, but the name 'Wreck Rock' and depth 22m do not match. Barco Hundido is at ~12m and is clearly documented. Cannot confirm this as a distinct site. Retaining as unvalidated pending local operator confirmation.

No site-specific sources found for 'Punta Juan' as a dive site. The geographic point (Punta Juan Brown on getamap.net at 9.3333/-82.2000, western Isla Bastimentos) is confirmed as a real place, but no dive operator or dive report references this as a named dive site. Cannot validate site type, depth, or marine life. Retaining as unvalidated.

Zapatilla Cays confirmed as a dive site in Bastimentos National Marine Park. Pristine reef confirmed. therealdealtours notes avg 40ft/12m, max varies; bocas-del-toro.org notes shallow protected site (20ft max). Caves/caverns also present per therealdealtours. Reef type confirmed as primary character. Alnaturalresort.com (Zapatillas Dive Center) confirms small-group diving at protected reefs around the cays.

Dolphin Bay (Laguna Bocatorito) is a real bay 12km south of Bocas Town. Resident bottlenose dolphins confirmed year-round. However, multiple sources indicate swimming is restricted/not allowed in parts of the bay. No source found that describes Dolphin Bay as a scuba diving site with reef at 10m. It is overwhelmingly described as a snorkeling/boat tour experience. Cannot confirm as a dive site. Depth 10m unverified. Site type 'reef' unverified. Marine life descriptions in markdown are speculative for diving. Retaining as unvalidated — may need to be recategorized or removed.

No site-specific sources found for 'Long Cay Wall' as a named dive site. 'Manuel's Wall' is a documented deep wall dive on Isla Solarte (15-35m, advanced). 'Long Cay Wall' does not appear in any dive operator listing, ScubaBoard, or dive database. Cannot validate. Retaining as unvalidated — the wall dive character is plausible for this region but the site name is unconfirmed.

tags.name: Barco Hundido
tags.qa_notes: CORRECTED: depth 17m -> 12m (multiple sources consistently state ~12m/40ft, NOT 17m). CORRECTED: difficulty Intermediate -> Beginner (12m max, intentionally sunk artificial reef, calm sheltered waters — accessible all levels). Vessel: old ferryboat, intentionally sunk ~1997 to create artificial reef. Location: SW side of Isla Colon, confirmed by multiple sources. No vessel name or tonnage found in any source — all historical wreck claims stripped from markdown. Marine life: hogfish, nurse sharks, reef fish, seahorses confirmed by sources.
